description_long: This is a classic ball-peen hammer featuring a robust, rounded wooden handle and a dual-ended metal head. The head consists of a flat striking face on one side and a rounded, ball-shaped peen on the other, both showing signs of use and darkened patina. The wooden handle is slightly curved, with a visibly worn texture and a natural brown coloration, suggesting frequent handling. The metal head is dark, likely steel, and exhibits some surface wear and minor discoloration, indicating age and repeated impact. No visible branding or decorative elements are present, giving the hammer a utilitarian, workmanlike appearance. This tool is typical for metalworking, carpentry, or general repair tasks in urban garages or workshops.
description: A well-used ball-peen hammer with a worn wooden handle and a dark, dual-ended metal head, showing signs of age and frequent use.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full length of the wooden handle and the symmetrical ball-peen head, with both striking faces visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the slim profile of the handle and the thickness of the metal head, with the ball and flat faces clearly distinguished.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the handle's curvature and the rounded ball-peen end, with the flat face partially visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the handle's length and the head's dual ends from the other side.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a natural finish, visibly worn and textured for grip.
attribute_head_type: Ball-peen hammer head with one flat striking face and one rounded ball end, made of darkened steel.
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: Visible wear on the handle, darkened patina and minor surface marks on the metal head.
